avoid_rules.rule: NEVER make both halves of a split dark. At least one half must have bright, vivid, high-contrast elements. If the background is dark, the subject MUST pop with bright saturated color — not dark red on dark background.
examples: dark underwater with dark red elements
dark muddy palette where nothing is distinguishable

avoid_rules.rule: In split compositions, BOTH halves must have strong visual content. Never leave one half empty, plain, or minimal. A closed laptop, blank wall, or empty dark space wastes 50% of the thumbnail.
examples: left half is just a closed laptop on a desk
right half is completely dark with nothing visible
left half is empty white space

avoid_rules.rule: Every thumbnail MUST have one element your eye goes to instantly. If someone glances at it for 0.5 seconds at 320px, they should see ONE clear thing. Generic busy compositions with no hierarchy fail.
examples: wall of monitors with no clear subject
generic soldier silhouettes with nothing standing out
busy split where both sides compete equally

avoid_rules.rule: Avoid monochromatic dark palettes. Every thumbnail needs at least TWO clearly different colors with high contrast between them. Red-on-black only works if the red is BRIGHT and SATURATED, not dark maroon.
examples: everything is dark red and black — no contrast
dark blue underwater with dark red dots — invisible
